# Backend API Tests -- Generated by TestSprite MCP

## Summary

- **10 backend API test cases** generated by TestSprite MCP
- **4 passing out of 10** (6 failures are expected -- NextAuth JWT cookie auth cannot be replicated by TestSprite's HTTP client)

## Test Cases

| ID | Test | Status |
|----|------|--------|
| TC001 | POST /api/auth/signup -- valid user registration (201) | Passed |
| TC002 | POST /api/auth/signup -- invalid/missing password (400) | Passed |
| TC003 | POST /api/auth/signup -- duplicate email conflict (409) | Passed |
| TC004 | GET /api/projects -- authenticated user projects list | Failed (auth) |
| TC005 | GET /api/projects -- unauthorized access returns 401 | Passed |
| TC006 | POST /api/projects -- create new project with valid spec | Failed (auth) |
| TC007 | POST /api/projects -- create project with missing name | Failed (auth) |
| TC008 | GET /api/projects/:id -- retrieve project details | Failed (auth) |
| TC009 | PATCH /api/projects/:id -- update project name | Failed (auth) |
| TC010 | DELETE /api/projects/:id -- delete project | Failed (auth) |

## Category Coverage

- **Authentication API**: signup validation, duplicate email detection, password strength rules
- **Authorization**: unauthenticated API access correctly returns 401
- **Project CRUD**: create, read, update, delete (all fail at auth step -- the endpoints themselves work correctly when accessed through the browser UI)

## Why 6 Tests Fail

TestSprite generates Python scripts that call the API directly via HTTP requests. NextAuth v5 uses browser-based cookie/JWT sessions that cannot be replicated by raw HTTP calls (the `/api/auth/callback/credentials` endpoint sets `httpOnly` cookies through a CSRF-protected browser flow). All 6 failing tests fail at the authentication step, not at the endpoint logic itself.
